code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 0)
text:
THIS PRINT COVERS CALENDAR ITEM NO. 12A 
 
 
 
MEMORANDUM 
 
 November 14, 2025 
 
TO: 
 
MEMBERS, PORT COMMISSION 
Hon. Gail Gilman, President 
 
 
 
Hon. Stephen Engblom, Vice President 
Hon. Willie Adams 
Hon. Steven Lee 
 
Hon. Ken McNeely 
 
FROM: 
Elaine Forbes  
Executive Director 
 
SUBJECT: Informational presentation to consider and possible action to approve 
amendments to the 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code. 
 
 
DIRECTOR'S RECOMMENDATION:  Approve the Attached Resolution No. 25-80  
 
 
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
 
Every three years, the State of California Building Standards Commission updates the 
California Building Standards Code. The State’s Building Code must be adopted and 
enforced by local agencies, and local agencies may add their own local amendments. The 
Port of San Francisco is the local building department within our jurisdiction, performing a 
similar but separate function to the San Francisco Department of Building Inspection’s 
(DBI) role in the rest of San Francisco. Staff have updated the Port’s local amendments 
with a focus on avoiding new regulations, clarifying ambiguous language, and maximizing 
consistency with DBI’s local amendments. The combination of the California Building 
Standards Code and the Port’s local amendments is known as the Port of San Francisco 
Building Standards Code. Once adopted, the 2025 Port of San Francisco Building 
Standards Code will become effective on January 1st, 2026. 
 
 
 
 
-2- 
STRATEGIC OBJECTIVE  
 
The proposed adoption of the 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code 
supports the following strategic objectives: 
 
Economic Growth  
The Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code maintains permit fees at current levels 
for at least the remainder of this fiscal year, supporting our tenant businesses and project 
partners, while still covering the Port’s operational costs. 
 
Resilience   
The Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code includes the latest State-level

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 1)
text:
rting our tenant businesses and project 
partners, while still covering the Port’s operational costs. 
 
Resilience   
The Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code includes the latest State-level 
regulations for design of buildings and other regulated structures to protect against the 
effects of earthquakes, floods, fires, and other environmental hazards, while also reducing 
the climate impacts of building construction and operation. 
 
Sustainability    
The Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code incorporates the latest State-level and 
City-level regulations for green building design and operations, including energy efficiency 
standards, water conservation standards, and promotion of renewable energy sources 
such as rooftop solar. 
 
BACKGROUND 
 
The California Building Standards Commission, in accordance with the California Health 
and Safety Code, establishes construction standards in California which are codified in the 
California Code of Regulations, Title 24, also known as the “California Building Standards 
Code” (referred to as the “State Code”). The State Code consists of regulations governing 
the design and construction of buildings, structures, and associated infrastructure. The 
State Code is updated every three years. The current 2022 State Code became effective 
January 1st, 2023, and the updated 2025 State Code will become effective on January 1st, 
2026. 
 
Local jurisdictions in California must adopt the latest State Code, but State law allows for 
more restrictive local amendments that are reasonably necessary because of local 
climatic, geological, or topographical conditions. The Port of San Francisco is a geographic 
jurisdiction separate from the jurisdiction of the DBI. The combination of the State Code 
and the Port’s local amendments is collectively referred to as the Port of San Francisco 
Building Standards Code. 
 
The Port of San Francisco’s local amendments primarily consist of special design 
requirements to addres

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 2)
text:
mendments is collectively referred to as the Port of San Francisco 
Building Standards Code. 
 
The Port of San Francisco’s local amendments primarily consist of special design 
requirements to address the Port’s proximity to San Francisco Bay waters, its unique 
topographical and geographical conditions, and its unique jurisdictional structure. The 
Port’s local amendments also closely mirror local amendments in the San Francisco 
Building Code administered by DBI. Mirroring DBI’s amendments results in consistency of 
design and construction regulations within the City of San Francisco, and incorporates 
local ordinances passed by the Board of Supervisors that apply City-wide. 
-3- 
 
Staff are seeking Port Commission approval to adopt the State Code and the Port’s local 
amendments. This will enable the continuance of the Port’s current administrative 
procedures, submittal requirements, and construction standards applicable to the Port’s 
marine environment, facilities, and maritime operations. 
 
The California Building Standards Commission has adopted a new 2025 edition of the 
State Code. The new edition will supersede the current 2022 edition of the State Code in 
its entirety and will have an effective date of January 1, 2026. As a result, the 2022 edition 
of the Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code, which was based on the 2023 
edition of the State Code, will be outdated as of January 1, 2026. If the Port Commission 
does not adopt the updated Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code, the Port’s 
construction standards will, as of January 1, 2026, default to the 2025 State Code without 
Port jurisdictional amendments. 
 
PORT OF SAN FRANCISCO AMENDMENTS 
 
In this code revision cycle, staff took the approach of minimizing any new requirements 
above and beyond our current amendments. Changes were primarily to clarify existing 
Port of San Francisco local amendments and to align with any changes to DBI’s 
amendments wherever possible. 
 
Appea

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 3)
text:
ove and beyond our current amendments. Changes were primarily to clarify existing 
Port of San Francisco local amendments and to align with any changes to DBI’s 
amendments wherever possible. 
 
Appeals: Permit applicants have the right to appeal staff’s interpretation or application of 
the code. In consultation with the Office of the City Attorney, the Port Building Code 
language regarding appeals has been modified to state that the Port Commission has 
decision-making authority for such appeals. No formal code appeals have been lodged 
against the Port in at least the past three years. The lack of appeals has made it 
impractical for the Port to maintain a volunteer appeals board of qualified professionals as 
was required by previous Code editions. Staff work closely and proactively with permit 
applicants to discuss and resolve code interpretation questions. In the unlikely event of a 
future appeal, the Commission would have several options, including: 
 
• Hearing the appeal directly 
• Establishing an ad-hoc subcommittee of qualified professionals to hear the appeal 
and advise the Commission 
• Potentially utilizing DBI’s appeals boards through a future MOU, to hear the appeal 
and advise the Commission 
 
Staff have begun discussions with DBI on an MOU to utilize DBI’s appeals boards should 
the need arise. 
 
No change to fees: The Port assesses fees to permit applicants to recover our costs for 
administering building permits, and our fee schedule is included in the Port Building Code. 
Our fees are primarily based on a percentage of the total valuation of the project, so as 
construction costs increase, our fees increase. Historically, Port building permit fees were 
based on DBI’s fee schedule. In the 2022 San Francisco Building Code, DBI increased its 
percentage fees and fixed value fees, but the Port did not duplicate DBI’s increases. On 
initial adoption, the 2025 Port Building Code maintains our current fee schedule, 
-4- 
supporting our tenant b

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 4)
text:
ercentage fees and fixed value fees, but the Port did not duplicate DBI’s increases. On 
initial adoption, the 2025 Port Building Code maintains our current fee schedule, 
-4- 
supporting our tenant businesses and other project partners. Currently, the Port’s fee 
intake for building permits recovers our direct staff and software costs for administering 
building permits, excluding the Port’s own capital projects, which are not assessed fees. 
Port Engineering and Finance will continue to assess permit fee intake versus internal 
costs each fiscal year and will request future Commission authorization if fee increases 
become justified and necessary. 
 
Under-pier plumbing: The 2025 Port Plumbing Code allows a broader range of materials 
suitable for under-pier piping exposed to the marine environment, as compared to previous 
Port amendments. This modification to the Port’s code amendments was made after 
lessons learned from recent Port projects with under-pier plumbing. 
 
IMPROVEMENTS TO THE APPLICANT EXPERIENCE 
 
In parallel with the development and publication of the 2025 Port Building Code, staff are 
working to improve the experience of applicants on several fronts. 
 
• Simplification of the Port’s Permit Services Webpage, and inclusion of step-by-step 
guides for common permitting tasks. 
 
• Revisions to the Port Code Procedures, which provide additional guidance to 
architects, engineers, and other construction professionals on specific code 
sections. 
 
• Developing a uniform set of requirements and application processes City-wide for 
permitting Special Events in coordination with the Mayor’s PermitSF initiative. 
 
SCHEDULE OF NEXT STEPS 
 
The draft 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code is posted to the Port’s 
website, and mandatory public notices were published ten days prior to the hearing date. 
The November 18th Port Commission meeting serves as the mandatory public hearing 
prior to the adoption of these codes. After public review

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 5)
text:
notices were published ten days prior to the hearing date. 
The November 18th Port Commission meeting serves as the mandatory public hearing 
prior to the adoption of these codes. After public review and resolution of any comments 
received, staff plans to request Port Commission approval and adoption of the proposed 
2025 Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code and associated local findings. Upon 
adoption of the 2025 edition of the Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code, staff 
will file the local findings and associated code amendments with the California Building 
Standards Commission to finalize the process. The adopted 2025 Port of San Francisco 
Building Standards Code and associated local findings will have an effective date of 
January 1, 2026. 
 
 
-5- 
SUMMARY 
 
Staff have produced a draft of the proposed 2025 Port of San Francisco Building 
Standards Code with local findings and posted the draft code for public review and 
comment. Staff will present the draft of the 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Standards 
Code at the November 18, 2025 Port Commission meeting for consideration and possible 
approval. The 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code will, upon review 
approval and adoption, repeal the 2022 edition of the Port of San Francisco Building 
Standards Code and will be effective as of January 1, 2026. 
 
 
Prepared by:  
Matthew Bell, Acting Chief Harbor Engineer 
Engineering Division 
 
For: 
 
 
Wendy Proctor, Deputy Director 
Engineering Division 
 
 
-6- 
PORT COMMISSION 
CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO 
 
RESOLUTION NO. 25-80 
 
 
WHEREAS,  The Port of San Francisco derives its authority to regulate and permit 
building construction and improvements within its jurisdiction from the Burton 
Act (Statutes 1968, Chapter 1333) and relevant sections of the Agreement 
Relating to Transfer of the Port of San Francisco from the State of California 
to the City and County of San Francisco; and 
 
WHEREAS,  The Port has e

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 6)
text:
r 1333) and relevant sections of the Agreement 
Relating to Transfer of the Port of San Francisco from the State of California 
to the City and County of San Francisco; and 
 
WHEREAS,  The Port has established the Building Permit Services group as part of the 
Port Engineering Division with the responsibility for enforcement, 
administration, and interpretation of building standards applicable to Port 
property; and 
 
WHEREAS,  The Port Engineering Division is considered a local building department; and 
 
WHEREAS, Through Resolution 22-65, the Port Commission adopted (1) the 2022 Port of 
San Francisco Building Code, (2) 2022 Port of San Francisco Existing 
Building Code (3) 2022 Port of San Francisco Mechanical Code, (4) 2022 
Port of San Francisco Electrical Code, and (5) 2022 Port of San Francisco 
Plumbing Code, and (6) 2022 Port of San Francisco Green Building 
Standards Code based upon the 2022 California Building Standards Code 
(codified in Title 24 of the California Code of Regulations) collectively known 
as the 2022 Port of San Francisco Building Standards Code; and 
 
WHEREAS,  The California Building Standards Commission has adopted a new 2025 
edition of the California Building Standards Code (referred to also as the 
“2025 State Code”), which takes effect on January 1, 2026; and 
 
WHEREAS,  The California Building Standards Law (Health and Safety Code Section 
18901 and following) mandates that local jurisdictions within the State apply 
the 2025 State Code, provided that a local jurisdiction may make 
modifications to the State Code it determines are reasonably necessary 
because of local climatic, geological or topographical conditions as specified 
by California Health and Safety Code Section 17958.5 and 17958.7; and 
 
WHEREAS,  In response to that requirement, the Port engineering staff have prepared 
and drafted the (1) 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Code, (2) 2025 Port 
of San Francisco Existing Building Code, (3) 2025 Port of San Fran

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 7)
text:
t requirement, the Port engineering staff have prepared 
and drafted the (1) 2025 Port of San Francisco Building Code, (2) 2025 Port 
of San Francisco Existing Building Code, (3) 2025 Port of San Francisco 
Mechanical Code, (4) 2025 Port of San Francisco Electrical Code, (5) 2025 
Port of San Francisco Plumbing Code, and (6) 2025 Port of San Francisco 
Green Building Standards Code (collectively referred to as the "2025 Port of 
San Francisco Building Standards Code"), which incorporate the 2025 State 
Code with the Port’s code amendments that are based on the Port’s local 
-7- 
climatic, geologic or topographical conditions, and the Port's administrative 
structure; and 
 
WHEREAS,  Port staff have published the draft 2025 Port of San Francisco Building 
Standards Code, based on the 2025 State Code, which has been available 
for public review and comment; and 
 
WHEREAS, Port staff now recommends that the Port Commission adopt the 2025 Port of 
San Francisco Building Standards Code; now, therefore be it 
 
RESOLVED, That the Port Commission hereby adopts the 2025 Port of San Francisco 
Building Standards Code; and be it further 
 
RESOLVED, The Port Commission authorizes and directs staff to file the 2025 Port of San 
Francisco Building Standards Code in the form on file with the Port 
Commission Secretary, with such changes as may be needed to address 
scriveners errors, and to file local findings that support the Port's 
amendments to the 2025 State Code based on local climatic, geologic or 
topographical conditions, with the California Building Standards Commission 
no later than December 31, 2025; and be it further 
 
RESOLVED, The Port Commission hereby declares that upon such filing, the 2025 Port of 
San Francisco Building Standards Code shall have an effective date of 
January 1, 2026, and shall supersede and repeal the 2022 Port of San 
Francisco Building Standards Code in its entirety. 
 
 
I hereby certify that the foregoing resolution was adopted by the

code_name: Port of San Francisco 2025 Building Code Amendments (Ord. 02-05,10-11)
title: port_of_san_francisco_2025 (window 8)
text:
date of 
January 1, 2026, and shall supersede and repeal the 2022 Port of San 
Francisco Building Standards Code in its entirety. 
 
 
I hereby certify that the foregoing resolution was adopted by the Port Commission 
at its meeting of November 18, 2025. 
 
_____________________________ 
                                                                                                Secretary
